steps.title: Check current vllm setup
steps.description:
Verify your vllm version and configuration:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
```
This feature requires a working vllm installation.

steps.title: Review vllm configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vllm configuration:
### 🚀 The feature, motivation and pitch

vllm requires transformers<5, but some new models already require transformers 5.0.0rc0.
The combination of vllm>=0.12 and transformers>=5.0.0rc0 cannot be resolved.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature]: Support transformers>=5
steps.description:
I'm running the following: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/examples/offline_inference/batch_llm_inference.py with nightly Ray and vllm 0.15.1. I force upgrade transformers to 5.0.0, and run the same snippet and the code will break (after fixing the dependency of TRANSFORMERS_CACHE in Ray. Replace all of 
```python
from transformers.utils.hub import TRANSFORMERS_CACHE
```
with
```python
from huggingface_hub.constants import default_cache_path as TRANSFORMERS_CACHE
```
since

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade vllm to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade vllm:
```bash
# Check current version
pip show vllm | grep Version
# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature]: Support transformers>=5" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I'm running the following: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/examples/offline_inference/batch_llm_inference.py with nightly Ray and vllm 0.15.1. I force upgrade transformers to 5.0.0, and run the same snippet and the code will break (after fixing the dependency of TRANSFORMERS_CACHE in Ray.

prerequisites.description: A working vllm installation or development environment.
